  • His timeline in Willowshore (arriving ~2 months before the magistrate) raises questions about the ritual's timeline

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Radiant Willow shared additional details from her conversations with Voss:
    • He described "five lines, five fortunes" converging at Willowshore—aligning with the ley line convergence Migo revealed
    • He said "something is being drawn here"—the only thing about his research that seemed to worry him
    • His last known plans were to head east, following the rivers where the ley lines tracked
    • He had originally wanted to go north into the Dark Woods, but Willow warned him against it
    • Willow described him as "an amazing researcher" who was "so excited" about his findings—she still believes he simply moved on

docs.title: Hong
docs.text: A boy with firework dreams and a bag full of someone else's gold.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Hong Age ~10 years old Ancestry Kitsune Occupation Blacksmith's son / apprentice Location Eternal Blaze Ironworks, Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Hong is Yong's young son—a kitsune boy of about ten years old, energetic and impulsive. He has a restless spirit and dreams of leaving Willowshore for open skies and wide seas, far from the dense forests and small-town life he's known.

Despite his youth, Hong is perceptive enough to recognize opportunity and desperate enough to take dangerous risks for his family's sake.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Hong is earnest, loyal to his father, and terrible at lying. When confronted by the party at Silver Mist Lodge, his attempts at deception were transparent—darting eyes, stammered excuses, and a visible urge to flee. Once he felt safe enough to talk, his words tumbled out with the unfiltered honesty of a child who knows he's in over his head.

He is not malicious. The warehouse fire was meant to be a "prank"—he genuinely did not intend for anyone to get hurt. He invited Kimmy along to "see the fireworks," not realizing the danger.

docs.title: The Warehouse Job
docs.text:
Hong was hired by the Yeshou family to destroy the fireworks stored for the Festival of Returning Spirits. The details:

  • Payment: 100 gold pieces
  • Instructions: Cause a "prank" that would disrupt the festival by destroying the fireworks
  • Method: Hong used kerosene to ensure all the boxes of fireworks would ignite at once, since lighting them individually seemed impractical
  • The girl: Kimmy was not part of the plan; Hong invited her along
  • The fire: Got out of control far beyond what Hong expected; the party rescued both children

Hong met with the Yeshou family alone. He does not know if they hired anyone else for similar jobs.

docs.title: Yong (Father)
docs.text: Hong idolizes his father and took the dangerous job specifically to help him. Yong has been talking about leaving Willowshore—he "sees dark skies ahead"—but lacks the funds to relocate and re-establish his forge elsewhere. The 100 gold would make that possible.

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: Hong is grateful to the party for rescuing him from the fire and for securing his payment from the Yeshou family. He trusts them, particularly Boone, with whom he had an extended conversation about life beyond Willowshore—open seas, wide skies, places far from forests.

docs.title: Boone
docs.text: While the rest of the party met with Migo, Hong stayed behind with Boone at Silver Mist Lodge. He was fascinated by the dwarf's stories of distant lands and showed little interest in mountains or forests, preferring tales of open coastlines and wide horizons.

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:
• Trapped in the burning warehouse along with Kimmy
  • Rescued by Littlefinger and Ginkgo
  • Whispered "It wasn't my fault" after being pulled free
  • Burns treated with Da Baishan's salve

docs.title: Session Two (2026-02-05)
docs.text:
• Arrived at Silver Mist Lodge with a satchel, tried to flee when he saw the party
  • Confessed to being hired to destroy the fireworks warehouse
  • Revealed the Yeshou family as the ones who paid him
  • Disclosed his father's plan to leave Willowshore
  • Asked by the party to check if other kids were hired for similar pranks
  • Received the full 100 gold payment, escorted safely to Yong's forge by the party
  • Helped Yong at the forge while the party headed north

docs.title: Session Three (2026-02-12)
docs.text:
• Visited by Da Baishan and Boone in the evening at the forge
  • Reported that none of his friends were hired for sabotage—all are excited about the festival
  • Confirmed he asked them directly about "pranks" and payments from the Yeshou family; all said no
  • Was intimidated one final time by Da Baishan (Intimidation 23)—stepped back with wide eyes and a quivering voice, but stuck to his story
  • Boone clapped him on the shoulder reassuringly; Hong flinched and scampered off to "help Dad clean up"

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Fell into an old dry well trying to retrieve Kimmy's broken festival lantern—her "first wish" lantern for the river ceremony
  • Suffered a gash on his leg from a ceremonial dagger buried in the mud
  • Was too embarrassed to tell his father; sent Kimmy to find Boone instead
  • Rescued by Da Baishan, who climbed down and tied a rope around him
  • Blushed visibly through his kitsune fur when Da Baishan suggested he needed "a kiss to feel better"—Kimmy obliged with a peck on the cheek
  • The broken lantern meant Kimmy couldn't make her wish at the festival

docs.title: Kimmy
docs.text: The girl who ran across town to find a hero for a boy in a well.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Kimmy Age ~10-12 years old Ancestry Human Occupation Child Location Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Kimmy is a young human girl, roughly ten to twelve years old—the same girl Boone rescued from the burning warehouse in Session One. She is brave enough to sprint across town to find help, and honest enough that her panic reads as genuine rather than performative.

Her family runs the Cloud Paper Lodge, a paper lantern shop whose fireworks were stored in the destroyed warehouse.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text: Kimmy is earnest and resourceful for her age. When Hong fell into the well, she didn't panic uselessly—she ran to find the one person she trusted to help: Boone, the dwarf who had already saved her once. She is loyal to Hong and clearly cares about him, though neither child seems ready to fully acknowledge what that means.

docs.title: Boone
docs.text: Kimmy trusts Boone implicitly after he rescued her from the warehouse fire. She sought him out by name when Hong needed help.

docs.title: Hong
docs.text: Hong and Kimmy are close friends with hints of a childhood crush. Hong broke her festival lantern—her "first wish" lantern for the river ceremony—while trying to retrieve it from the well for her. When Da Baishan suggested Hong needed "a kiss to feel better," Kimmy gave him a shy peck on the cheek.

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:
• Trapped in the burning warehouse with Hong
  • Rescued by Boone, who crashed through a window to deliver her to her mother

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Ran across town to find Boone, reporting that Hong had fallen into a well
  • Led Boone, Da Baishan, and Littlefinger to the old dry well
  • Hugged Hong with relief when he was pulled out
  • Kissed Hong on the cheek at Da Baishan's suggestion
  • Could not make her "first wish" at the festival because Hong had broken her lantern

docs.title: Liwen
docs.text: A florist who counts petals and keeps her opinions to herself.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Liwen Ancestry Human Occupation Florist Location Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Liwen is a middle-aged woman in her forties, with long hair streaked with gray and permanently dirt-stained fingertips from years of gardening. She runs a tiny shop in Willowshore—barely more than a cart—selling flowers, arrangements, and whatever she can grow or gather. She supplies Radiant Willow with flowers for Dew Drop Petals.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Liwen is a private, cautious person who keeps her opinions close. She does not gossip freely and tends to avoid discussing the oni or the political changes in town. This is not deception but self-preservation—she has lived in Willowshore for years, likes the way things are, and fears what the new leadership might change.

She trusts Donkey, who is one of her regular herb suppliers, and is more forthcoming with him than she might be with strangers.

docs.title: Views on the Oni
docs.text: Liwen is quietly worried about the transition of power. She doesn't believe the change will be for the better, but keeps these thoughts to herself. She fulfilled Magistrate Kurosawa's order without complaint and did not volunteer information about it—though she shared the details when Donkey asked directly.

docs.title: The Lily Order
docs.text:
Magistrate Kurosawa personally visited Liwen and ordered five dozen white lilies—sixty flowers traditionally associated with funerals. When she asked the purpose, he said only that it was "an oni custom" and declined to elaborate. The specific quantity (five dozen) echoes the recurring pattern of fives connected to the ley lines and the ritual.

Liwen found the order unusual and mentioned it to Radiant Willow. She fulfilled it, delivered the lilies to the magistrate, and chose not to ask further questions.

docs.title: Donkey
docs.text: Liwen and Donkey have a regular supplier relationship. He brings her herbs and new plant specimens from his foraging trips, and she trusts his judgment on botanical matters. She was forthcoming with him about the lily order when he visited.

docs.title: Radiant Willow
docs.text: Willow is one of Liwen's primary customers, purchasing flowers for Dew Drop Petals.

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Visited by Donkey at her shop; discussed business and the festival preparations
  • Revealed that Magistrate Kurosawa ordered five dozen white lilies (funeral flowers), claiming it was an "oni custom"
  • Expressed cautious worry about the change in leadership
  • Asked Donkey to bring back new buds from his next foraging trip

docs.title: Luda
docs.text: "I don't trust any of those demons as far as I could toss them."

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Luda Ancestry Human (elderly) Occupation Seamstress, herbalist, informal arcane supplier Location Luda's Shop, Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Luda is an older woman who runs a modest shop in Willowshore. Her official trade is seamstress work—clothing repair, fabric, patches, general goods—but she also deals in rare plants, mushrooms, and alchemical ingredients. She has enough knowledge of alchemy and arcane components to supply spell-casters with the specialized inks, powders, and botanical materials needed for scrolls, spell transcription, and other magical crafting.

She is not a wizard or a practitioner, but she knows what the practitioners need and where to find it.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text: Luda is blunt, suspicious of authority, and unapologetically hostile toward the oni presence in Willowshore. She does not mince words or soften her opinions. She views the entire oni regime as corrupt and self-serving.

docs.title: Donkey
docs.text: Luda is one of Donkey's regular suppliers for spell components—special inks, crushed fungi, and other arcane crafting materials. They have a transactional but trusting relationship. When Donkey asked her to report on Kurosawa's future purchases, she agreed without hesitation.

docs.title: Magistrate Kurosawa
docs.text: Kurosawa demanded arcane ingredients from Luda in high volume with extreme specificity. He paid her, but the interaction was cold and intimidating. She is deeply concerned about the purpose of the ingredients and considers the oni's presence in Willowshore a facade for something sinister.

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text:
"I don't trust any of those demons as far as I could toss them."

"They all are up to no good. There's something going on and I do not believe it's in any of our interests."

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Introduced as Donkey's regular supplier for arcane crafting components
  • Revealed that Magistrate Kurosawa demanded high-volume, highly specific arcane ingredients from her—she was compensated but disturbed by the cold interaction and the nature of the request
  • Believes the oni presence is a facade for a nefarious arcane purpose
  • Holds an extremely low opinion of all the oni, especially Kurosawa
  • Agreed to track and report any future purchases by the magistrate to Donkey

docs.title: Magistrate Kurosawa
docs.text: "I could make people do things. That's not what I want to do here."

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Kurosawa Title Magistrate Ancestry Oni Affiliation Shogun Tsuneni's regime Location Willowshore (temporarily at the Yeshou estate)

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Magistrate Kurosawa is a 7-foot-tall oni with striking blue skin and a fierce, commanding presence. He wears samurai-style lamellar armor and carries an enormous nodachi—a massive two-handed katana that most humans couldn't even lift. His very presence seems to command attention; he rarely needs to raise his voice.

He arrived in Willowshore a few days before Mayor Masru, taking up residence in the Yeshou family estate after displacing them. His stated purpose is to ensure the transfer of power proceeds smoothly.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Kurosawa projects extreme confidence bordering on arrogance—the certainty of someone who knows they hold all the power. Yet he is not simply a brute. He understands the value of diplomacy and soft power, preferring persuasion to force when possible.

He views the party as useful tools: outsiders without entrenched loyalties who can investigate without raising suspicions. He is direct about this arrangement—he does not pretend to be their friend.

"I could make people do things. That's not what I want to do here."

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
• Establish oni authority over Willowshore with minimal resistance
  • Identify and neutralize potential sources of dissent—currently suspects the party
  • The Gosembiki — Investigating haunted ruins called the Five Pointed Star (五芒星), 10–20 miles east of Willowshore; has recently traveled there
  • The Mourndusk Willow ritual — Conducting a summoning of the Mother of a Thousand Wings at the ancient tree in the Dark Woods
  • A device — Commissioned Yong to build an apparatus; a diagram matching it was found on a map marking the eastern ruins
  • Arcane stockpiling — Purchasing high-volume, specific arcane ingredients from Luda, a local alchemical supplier

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: Kurosawa recruited the party as his deputies, paying them 50 gold up front with 50 more promised upon successful completion of their duties. He sees them as expendable assets who can do the groundwork he cannot—moving among the common folk without inspiring the same fear and silence that follows him.

docs.title: The Yeshou Family
docs.text: He views the Willowshore Ruling Family as the most obvious threat to a smooth transition. He displaced them from their estate and suggested the party investigate them first.

docs.title: Mayor Masru
docs.text: Kurosawa serves as the advance guard for the incoming mayor. His role is to prepare Willowshore for the transfer of power.

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text:
"This explosion the day before our arrival is highly suspect."

"I need some deputies, some eyes on the ground, some folks who will... that I can hold accountable."

"Be careful what you wish for." — On the consequences of failure

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:
• Introduced as the blue-skinned oni magistrate who arrived ahead of the mayor
  • Recruited the party as deputies after witnessing their rescue of the children
  • Paid 50 gold up front; promised 50 more upon success
  • Suggested the Yeshou family as the primary suspects for the warehouse fire
  • Departed to prepare the road for Mayor Masru's arrival

docs.title: Session Two (2026-02-05)
docs.text:
• Not directly encountered, but Migo revealed he has been making repeated secret visits to the Mourndusk Willow in the Dark Woods north of Willowshore
  • Described by the Yeshou family as "accomplished in the arcane arts" and "not exactly what he seems"
  • His interest in the ancient tree raises questions about his true purpose in Willowshore

docs.title: Session Three (2026-02-12)
docs.text:
• Not directly encountered, but the party discovered physical evidence of his ritual at the Mourndusk Willow:
    • A ceremonial dagger with Toanin (oni infernal language) inscription: "Let ancient law be sealed in the hand that guides Willowshore"
    • A clay bowl of moth wings and blood stains—components of a summoning ritual
    • A lichen blight spreading from the ritual site, infecting nearby wildlife
  • Da Baishan's Imperial Lore (24) confirmed "the hand that guides" is a reference to the oni magistrate
  • Migo identified the ritual as a summoning of the Mother of a Thousand Wings—an ancient pestilence demon—and stated Kurosawa is likely "well versed" in ley line magic and chose Willowshore specifically for its arcane convergence point
  • The party now possesses the ritual dagger and bowl as evidence

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Ordered five dozen white lilies (funeral flowers) from Liwen the florist, claiming it was "an oni custom"—refused to elaborate further
  • Sat beside Mayor Masru at the Festival of Returning Spirits transfer ceremony
  • Reacted intensely to the moth swarm that destroyed five front lanterns: locked eyes with Migo for a prolonged moment, then spoke urgently to the mayor and rushed back to the estate
  • His bedroom at the Yeshou estate contains papers written in Chthonic (an infernal script)—the same language as the Mourndusk Willow dagger—along with diagrams
  • Employs a fox construct as a security system—a carved stone-and-wood fox with glowing runes and blue eyes that howls an alarm when intruders are detected
  • Stormed into the estate after the fox alarm sounded, arriving while the party was still inside
  • Appeared frustrated with Mayor Masru's lack of urgency throughout the evening

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Burst into the master bedroom seconds after Donkey escaped as a firefly; noticed the fox alarm missing and papers disturbed on the desk
  • Confronted Da Baishan at the front door—accepted his story about responding to the alarm (Deception 12) but was not fully convinced; dismissed him and inspected the shed (whose door Da Baishan had broken in Session Four)
  • Did not notice Littlefinger hiding in the first-floor hallway or the missing medal from the glass display case
  • Returned to the festival roughly 30 minutes later; stood at the edge studying the crowd before approaching Mayor Masru and convincing him to retire for the evening
  • Declared martial law across Willowshore—oni guards nailed decrees to buildings and posts: no travel, no gatherings, suspects dealt with harshly
  • Personally patrolled the north side of town during the lockdown
  • Confronted the party when they approached him, saying: "I believe our criminals are within my sight. I think we'll reach them quickly."
  • Told Da Baishan he would summon him the following morning for investigative assistance—likely an interrogation
  • Had the party escorted back to Silver Mist Inn under guard
  • Migo revealed he has been investigating ruins east of town called the Gosembiki (Five Pointed Star) and has recently traveled there
  • Luda reported he demanded high-volume, specific arcane ingredients from her—further evidence of an active ritual or construction project

docs.title: Mayor Masru
docs.text: The oni mayor claiming authority over a village that has governed itself for generations.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Masru Title Mayor of Willowshore Ancestry Oni Affiliation Shogun Tsuneni's regime Status In office; residing at the Yeshou estate

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Masru is not the imposing figure one might expect from an oni mayor. He is blue-skinned like Magistrate Kurosawa, but that is where the resemblance ends. Where the magistrate is seven feet of lean menace, Masru is perhaps five-ten—short for an oni—and enormously round. He wears no armor. Folds of blue skin roll over his waistline. He lumbers rather than walks, and his first instinct upon arriving in Willowshore was to ask about the food.

He is flanked by four red-skinned oni guards in full samurai armor, each dwarfing him in size and martial bearing. They seem more like babysitters than bodyguards.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Masru is affable, approachable, and primarily motivated by appetite. He is easily distracted by food and drink, chatty with commoners, and seemingly unconcerned with the formalities of power. He told Boone and Ginkgo: "You can call me Mazrou. We don't need to be so formal."

Whether this is genuine friendliness or a carefully cultivated persona remains to be seen. His relationship with Magistrate Kurosawa appears tense—the magistrate glared at him when he wandered back to the festival to eat, and Masru gave the magistrate a pointed look when speaking about enjoying Willowshore's food and drink.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
For generations, Willowshore was too small and poor to warrant attention from the oni regime. The village governed itself under the de facto rule of a local family. Now, seemingly out of nowhere, an oni mayor has been appointed to take control.

According to oni custom, Mayor Masru arrived claiming to have "always been here"—the pretense that oni authority is eternal and uninterrupted, even when they've been absent for decades.

docs.title: Arrival
docs.text:
Masru arrived the evening of the Festival of Returning Spirits, walking the procession route from the south gate across the bridge to the Seven Colored Songbird Theater. The townspeople averted their eyes per custom until he passed, then followed behind.

At the theater, Migo performed a formal tea transfer ceremony: she poured, they drank, they bowed, and the transfer of power was complete. Masru sat heavily in his front-row seat, his four guards beside him, with the magistrate at his side.

After the ceremonial dance, when moths destroyed five lanterns and the magistrate rushed back to the estate, Masru's priority was clear: "But I didn't get to eat yet." He was lured back to the festival by Boone and Ginkgo with a basket of fish, and proceeded to feast enthusiastically—grabbing food, sampling drinks from other people's hands, and complaining about how terrible the travel rations had been.

docs.title: Magistrate Kurosawa
docs.text: The dynamic is clearly strained. Kurosawa serves as Masru's advance guard, but appears frustrated by the mayor's lack of urgency. Masru, for his part, seems content to let the magistrate handle security while he enjoys himself.

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: Masru responded warmly when Boone and Ginkgo introduced themselves with food. He expressed interest in meeting the citizens of Willowshore: "We'll be here for some sustained time."

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text:
"But I didn't get to eat yet."

"You can call me Mazrou. We don't need to be so formal."

"We'll be here for some sustained time."

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:
• Mentioned as the incoming mayor; arrival expected the next evening
  • The party was recruited by Magistrate Kurosawa to ensure his arrival goes smoothly

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• First appearance: Arrived during the Festival of Returning Spirits with four red-skinned oni guards
  • Physically unimposing—short for an oni, enormously round, unarmored
  • Completed the tea transfer ceremony with Migo, officially assuming power over Willowshore
  • Left the festival when the magistrate insisted after the moth incident, but was lured back by Boone and Ginkgo with food
  • Proved affable and approachable; introduced himself casually to the party
  • Feasted enthusiastically at the post-ceremony meal, sampling everything in sight

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Continued mingling at the festival after the ceremony—eating, drinking, and charming the townsfolk; described as "one of the crew" by the locals
  • His four guards remained present but silent and detached; they never ate or drank
  • Magistrate Kurosawa arrived at the festival, studied the crowd, then convinced Masru to retire for the evening; Masru was reluctant but acquiesced
  • Gave a brief, warm farewell to the townspeople: "I'm sure we'll have many more opportunities to get to know each other."
  • Departed with Kurosawa and guards to the estate
  • Co-signed the martial law decree alongside the magistrate—though Migo suggested Kurosawa is the one driving the investigation
  • Townspeople spoke mostly favorably of him after his departure, though some dismissed his friendliness as "day one of his act"

docs.title: Meilin
docs.text: The gravekeeper who danced, and made a town forget she'd ever worn tatters.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Meilin Ancestry Unknown Occupation Gravekeeper Location Graveyard, Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Meilin is Willowshore's gravekeeper—normally seen in rough work clothes with matted hair, tending the cemetery grounds and maintaining the cricket cages that ward off spirits. She keeps a low profile and few in town pay her much attention.

That changed at the Festival of Returning Spirits, where she performed as one of five ceremonial dancers. After receiving a glow-up from Radiant Willow, Meilin stunned the entire town. Townspeople whispered that she might be the most beautiful young woman in Willowshore—a revelation that caught everyone by surprise except Willow, who had seen the potential all along.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text: Meilin is shy, reserved, and cautiously observant. She is grateful without being effusive, and measures her words carefully. When asked about the new mayor, she expressed guarded optimism while noting she "can't ignore the recent peculiarities"—a sign of quiet intelligence beneath her modest demeanor.

docs.title: Littlefinger
docs.text: Littlefinger helped Meilin at the graveyard during Session Two, assisting with the cricket cages after the undead encounter. Radiant Willow noticed their interaction and considers them a potential match. Sadly, Littlefinger was infiltrating the Yeshou estate during Meilin's dance performance and missed her transformation entirely.

docs.title: Radiant Willow
docs.text: Willow gave Meilin her makeover for the festival and is clearly proud of the result. She considers the gravekeeper one of her finest works.

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Performed as one of five dancers at the Festival of Returning Spirits ceremonial dance
  • Her transformation after Willow's makeover was the talk of the town—previously unnoticed, now considered possibly the most beautiful young woman in Willowshore
  • Littlefinger was absent during her performance (he was swimming through a koi pond tunnel at the time)

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Radiant Willow arranged for Meilin to stand next to Littlefinger at the festival—she was embarrassed but willing
  • Thanked the party again for helping during the undead crisis and for the graveyard cleanup
  • Spoke about the mayor: cautiously optimistic but reserving judgment—"I can't ignore some of the recent peculiarities. We had an undead uprising."
  • Described as having the "classic 80s rom-com glow-up"—her previously matted hair and rough clothes replaced by festival finery
  • Littlefinger expressed regret at missing her dance; she was pleased but still shy about the attention

docs.title: Migo
docs.text: The old woman smokes, drinks, and runs Willowshore's most powerful family from a borrowed room in a faded inn.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Migo Title Matriarch / Elder Ancestry Human (elderly) Affiliation Willowshore Ruling Family (Yeshou family) Location Silver Mist Lodge, Willowshore (temporarily)

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Migo is the matriarch of the Yeshou family—the de facto ruling family of Willowshore. She is elderly, possibly ancient, having been alive during the family's founding era. She holds court in a darkened suite at Silver Mist Lodge, curtains drawn, wreathed in tobacco smoke, flanked by two guards who are family retainers rather than blood relatives.

She has a piercing gaze and projects quiet, absolute authority. She drinks and smokes with the unhurried ease of someone who has outlasted most of her problems.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Migo is sharp, pragmatic, and honest when it serves her interests. She does not posture or waste words. When confronted with the party's evidence of her family's sabotage, she admitted it immediately—calling it a "foolish endeavor" by "more brash" members—rather than wasting time on denials.

She values honesty in return. Da Baishan's straightforward admission that the party was working under the magistrate's orders, but unwillingly, earned her respect and cooperation.

She is protective of her family's legacy and deeply opposed to the oni takeover, but she understands when a gambit has failed and cuts her losses cleanly.

docs.title: Goals
docs.text:
• Preserve the Yeshou family's influence in Willowshore—family has voted to stay permanently
  • Monitor Magistrate Kurosawa's activities, particularly his expeditions to the eastern ruins (Gosembiki)
  • Support the party's investigation while maintaining plausible deniability
  • Understand and counter the magistrate's arcane plans for Willowshore

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: Migo was impressed by the party's honesty and directness. She shared intelligence about Kurosawa's movements and honored the 100 gold payment to Hong as promised. She expressed hope for "further conversations and a deeper understanding" with the group.

docs.title: Magistrate Kurosawa
docs.text: She views him with deep suspicion. Her family has been tracking his movements and discovered his repeated visits to the Mourndusk Willow in the Dark Woods. She warned the party that he is "accomplished in the arcane arts" and "not exactly what he seems."

docs.title: Littlefinger
docs.text: She knows Littlefinger through his family connection. She apologized to him specifically for his injuries in the fire, calling them "scratches" with dry understatement.

docs.title: Hong
docs.text: She took advantage of Hong's desperation, hiring a child to do dangerous work. She acknowledged this was dishonorable and chose to honor the payment regardless.

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text:
"I will give you the name. And it will—I'll say myself. I will take the blame for this endeavor."

"Like many things here in Tian Xia, [Kurosawa] is not exactly what he seems."

"I would venture in the daytime. The Dark Woods have their name because of the dangers in the dark."

docs.title: Session Two (2026-02-05)
docs.text:
• Introduced as the Yeshou family matriarch in Silver Mist Lodge
  • Admitted to sponsoring the warehouse sabotage via Hong
  • Promised to cancel all further planned disruptions to the festival
  • Honored the 100 gold payment to Hong
  • Revealed Kurosawa's secret visits to the Mourndusk Willow in the Dark Woods
  • Warned the party about Kurosawa's arcane abilities
  • Established a cautious rapport with the party

docs.title: Session Three (2026-02-12)
docs.text:
• Received Ginkgo and Donkey again for a second audience; served tea and rice cakes
  • Praised the party as "the young heroes of Willowshore"
  • Analyzed the ritual findings (dagger, bowl, lichen blight) and identified it as an old demonic summoning of the "Mother of a Thousand Wings"—an ancient demon of pestilence
  • Explained that Willowshore sits at a convergence of ley lines—arcane geomancy paths mirrored by the five rivers—creating a focal point for amplifying magical power
  • Revealed the Mourndusk Willow serves as a "lightning rod" for the ritual, reaching into ancient earth through its deep roots
  • Believes Kurosawa knows about the ley lines and that this is the true reason for his assignment to Willowshore
  • Admitted the ritual is beyond her knowledge to counter, but suggested two leads: searching the magistrate's quarters and exploring old mining towns in the southern mountains
  • Disclosed two secret entrances to the Yeshou estate: stables → wine cellar, and through the base of the koi pond
  • Mentioned the Festival of Returning Spirits is tomorrow evening; expects to see the party there

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Performed the tea transfer ceremony with Mayor Masru at the Seven Colored Songbird Theater, officially ceding power
  • Sat with her family on the right side of the front row; The Smiling One was present among the family
  • Locked eyes with Magistrate Kurosawa for a prolonged moment after the moth swarm destroyed five lanterns—both clearly understood its significance
  • Gathered her family and departed toward Silver Mist Lodge after the incident, slowed by townsfolk wanting to speak with her

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Received the party late at night after martial law was declared; served tea, smoking at her table
  • Revealed the Gosembiki (五芒星, the Five Pointed Star)—haunted ruins 10–20 miles east of Willowshore that Kurosawa has named and recently visited; described as a convergence point of arcane or chthonic intent
  • Her brother Shang translated the Chthonic name for her
  • Confirmed the pattern of fives: five ley lines, five lanterns destroyed, and the Five Pointed Star; said she does not believe it is coincidence
  • Explained the prolonged eye-lock with Kurosawa at the festival as a mutual acknowledgment—"He knows that we are aware of his current festivities"
  • Warned the party that Kurosawa is "an elusive enigma of arcane energies"—vastly knowledgeable in the arcane arts
  • Agreed to provide an alibi for the party: the family will say they were present for a family vote during the festival, departing only when the alarm sounded
  • Revealed the family voted to stay in Willowshore permanently after the ceremony: "This place, we've been here for generations. We can't leave it to the hands of chance."
  • Littlefinger returned Shang's military medal; Migo was appreciative but not leveraged by the gesture (Diplomacy 11)
  • Read as forthright and honest by Littlefinger (Perception—good roll)
  • Expressed belief that the land's spirits are unhappy: moths, infestations, and the ghoul uprising are signs of displeasure with the magistrate's activities

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Peony Switchstep Ancestry Halfling Occupation Seamstress Location Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Peony Switchstep is a halfling seamstress in Willowshore, known for her cheerful disposition and love of community gossip. Radiant Willow considers her an ideal match for Da Baishan—reasoning that his stern nature would be complemented by someone who sees the best in everyone.

docs.title: Da Baishan (potential)
docs.text: Willow has been trying to arrange a meeting. Peony has been visibly reluctant—giving Willow resistance when she tried to bring her over to meet Da Baishan at the festival.

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text: • Mentioned by Radiant Willow as a matchmaking candidate for Da Baishan

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text: • Present at the festival; Willow attempted to bring her to meet Da Baishan but she gave visible resistance

docs.title: Radiant Willow
docs.text: "Everything is a canvas for beauty."

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Radiant Willow (prefers "Willow") Ancestry Leshy (Flower) Occupation Beautician, stylist, spa owner Location Dew Drop Petals, Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Radiant Willow is a young, beautiful flower leshy who practically radiates enthusiasm. She runs Dew Drop Petals, a small salon and spa in Willowshore where she offers manicures, pedicures, hair styling, beard braiding, and fashion consulting. She's known for her singing and dancing, and will be performing at the Festival of Returning Spirits.

When she enters a room, she brings energy with her—skipping, singing lullabies, and immediately assessing everyone's beauty potential. She sees the world as a canvas waiting to be adorned.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Willow is irrepressibly cheerful, effusive, and persistent. She doesn't take "no" for an answer easily—at least not when it comes to offering her services. She believes passionately that everyone deserves to look beautiful, and she cannot understand why anyone would choose to be "drab."

Despite her bubbly exterior, she's an excellent listener. As she styles hair and files nails, people talk to her—and she hears all the gossip in town. She was coy about sharing what she knows, but hinted that a visit to her shop might loosen her tongue.

docs.title: Views on the Oni
docs.text: Willow is cautiously optimistic about the incoming mayor. She hopes the change might bring prosperity and give Willowshore something to smile about. She admires Magistrate Kurosawa's "gorgeous blue skin" but wishes he would smile more. She's reserving judgment until Mayor Masru actually arrives.

docs.title: Ginkgo
docs.text: Willow was delighted to meet a fellow leshy and responded warmly to his flattery. She scheduled him for a full makeover the morning before the festival, clearly excited at the prospect of working with fungal aesthetics.

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: She tried (and mostly failed) to interest the others in her services: - Da Baishan flatly refused to adorn his tusks - Boone was noncommittal about beard braiding - Littlefinger declined hair styling but expressed interest in pedicures

docs.title: Services
docs.text:
Dew Drop Petals offers:

  • Hair styling and braiding
  • Manicures and pedicures
  • Beard styling (she desperately wants to braid Boone's)
  • Tusk adornment (she desperately wants to cap Da Baishan's with rubies)
  • Fashion consulting
  • Cucumber water (complimentary)

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text:
"Now, if this isn't the most interesting group I've ever seen!"

"Littlefinger, your hands are dreadful. We could do wonders to soften those for you."

"It is manly to look beautiful."

"I will get you to adorn your tusks one day." — To Da Baishan

docs.title: Matchmaking
docs.text:
Willow considers herself an innate matchmaker—she believes finding love connections is one of her gifts, alongside dance and beauty. She has identified several potential pairings among the party and townsfolk:

Match Her Reasoning Da Baishan & Peony Switchstep "He's stern; she sees the best in everyone. Can you imagine the wedding on the bridge?" Boone & Cloud Ygritte "She's sweet, patient, likes trading stories. He's from a faraway land. And she's got the most adorable chin fuzz." Littlefinger & Meilin She noticed Littlefinger spent time helping Meilin at the graveyard and planned to watch his reaction to her dance

She recruited Ginkgo as her accomplice, planning to arrange "accidental" meetings at the festival feast.

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:

  • Scheduled Ginkgo for a makeover the next morning
  • Hinted she hears gossip from her customers
  • Mentioned she'll perform a dance at the festival
  • Attempted to recruit the entire party for beauty treatments

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:

  • Revealed matchmaking plans for Da Baishan, Boone, and Littlefinger with local women
  • Recruited Ginkgo as her matchmaking accomplice for the festival feast
  • Shared information about Cassian Voss: he told her about "five lines, five fortunes" converging at Willowshore and that "something is being drawn here"; he planned to head east following the ley lines
  • Gave fortune petals to Ginkgo and Donkey on their way out
  • Performed in the Festival of Returning Spirits ceremonial dance—graceful and radiant, a standout among the five dancers
  • Presented chrysanthemum blooms to Mayor Masru and Elder Migo to close the performance
  • Responded warmly to Ginkgo's romantic interest; agreed to get a drink together later

docs.text:

  • Attempted to bring Peony Switchstep to meet Da Baishan but met visible resistance
  • Cloud Ygritte (Boone's match) had already retired for the evening before introductions could be made

docs.title: Shogun Tsuneni
docs.text: The undisputed ruler of Chu Ye. No one has come close to challenging his power.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Tsuneni (TSOO-neh-nee) Title Shogun of Chu Ye Type Void Oni (Voidlord) Alignment Tyrannical Location Jyito (capital)

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Shogun Tsuneni is a void oni of immense power—the undisputed ruler of Chu Ye. He overthrew the old order when Imperial Lung Wa fell and has maintained his grip on the nation for a century. His rule is absolute; no challenger has ever come close to threatening his position.

He convenes a ruling council in Jyito once a year to discuss affairs of state—a lavish event designed to appeal to the hedonistic nature of oni, making attendance something officials desire rather than dread.

docs.title: Government
docs.text:
Tsuneni rules through two lieutenants who manage day-to-day governance:

  • Steward Imora (Storm Oni, Marshal) — Oversees the military
  • Steward Tanyo (Void Oni, Councilor) — Manages the bureaucracy

The ruling council contains several factions that Tsuneni enjoys pitting against each other:

Faction Leader Philosophy Daiyoku Uno (Caldera Oni) Militaristic; seeks to conquer all Tian Xia Shinkaze Shiga (Storm Oni) Isolationist; seeks spiritual redemption Hedonists Tamami (Village Oni) Pleasure-seekers; no interest in ruling

docs.title: Relevance to Willowshore
docs.text:
Willowshore has been without an oni mayor for generations—a small, forgotten village allowed to govern itself. Now, a new oni mayor is arriving, bringing Tsuneni's regime directly into the lives of the villagers for the first time in living memory.

The arrival has stirred unrest. The old ruling family refuses to participate in the traditional Revival of Friendship ceremony. Tensions are rising.

docs.title: Session Zero (2026-01-16)
docs.text:
• Referenced as the overarching ruler of Chu Ye
  • His regime's expansion into Willowshore established as a key tension

docs.title: The Smiling One — Shang Yesho
docs.text: "I know the real ending."

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Shang Yesho Title The Smiling One Ancestry Human Affiliation Willowshore Ruling Family Role Family member; Migo's brother; decorated military veteran Location Silver Mist Lodge, Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Shang Yesho—known around Willowshore as "The Smiling One"—is Migo's brother and a member of the Yeshou family. He is perpetually smiling, no matter the circumstances. When misfortune befalls the oni or their allies, his expression brightens. When the new regime stumbles, he seems quietly pleased. He is always in the background, always watching, always grinning.

He is a decorated military veteran who received a gold medal with gem inlays for bravery beyond the call of duty during his younger years. The medal was signed by a general and describes a distinguished act of valor.

docs.title: Knowledge
docs.text:
Shang has experience with the Chthonic language (the Devil's tongue) and was able to translate the term Gosembiki (五芒星, the Five Pointed Star) for his sister—the name Magistrate Kurosawa gave to haunted ruins east of Willowshore.

He also claims to know the true ending of the oldest story about the forest shadows—a tale whose ending is never spoken aloud because it embarrasses the ruling family.

docs.title: Migo
docs.text: His sister and the family matriarch. Shang serves as a trusted advisor and translator, particularly for matters involving the Chthonic language.

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: When Littlefinger returned his military medal, Shang came forward—still smiling—and took it back with obvious delight. He has not spoken directly to the party at length.

docs.title: Notable Quotes
docs.text: "I know the real ending."

docs.title: Session Zero (2026-01-16)
docs.text:
• Established through world-building as "someone who always smiles when the news is bad"
  • Connected to the ruling family and the secret ending of the forest story

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text: • Present among the Yeshou family at the Festival of Returning Spirits transfer ceremony

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Identified as Shang Yesho, Migo's brother and a military veteran
  • His military medal for bravery was found in a display case at the Yeshou estate (now occupied by Kurosawa) and stolen by Littlefinger
  • Littlefinger returned the medal to him; Shang took it with a smile and obvious delight
  • Translated the Chthonic term Gosembiki (Five Pointed Star) for Migo—the name the magistrate gave to ruins east of Willowshore
  • Confirmed to have experience with the Chthonic language

docs.title: Yong
docs.text: The blacksmith who owes the party a debt he can never fully repay.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Name Yong Ancestry Kitsune Occupation Blacksmith Location Eternal Blaze Ironworks, Willowshore

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Yong is a young kitsune in his late twenties—fit and strong from years of working the forge. He runs Eternal Blaze Ironworks, Willowshore's only blacksmith shop. While not a master weaponsmith, he can craft serviceable weapons, tools, farming implements, bridles, and barding. Armor is beyond his current skill.

When the party met him, Yong was exhausted and frustrated. Magistrate Kurosawa had been piling work orders on him in preparation for the festival, leaving him little time for regular customers.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text: Yong is hardworking and practical, but clearly stressed by the demands of the oni regime. He expressed relief when the party indicated they weren't there on behalf of the magistrate. He loves his son Hong deeply and was terrified when he learned the boy was trapped in the burning warehouse.

docs.title: Hong
docs.text:
Yong's young son, described as rambunctious but not troublesome. Hong was caught in the warehouse fire along with Kimmy. Despite his playful nature, he's learning the trade and helps at the forge when he can. Yong had planned to give him a set of shuriken he'd crafted.

It was later revealed that Hong was hired by the Yeshou family to set the warehouse fire—a "prank" for 100 gold pieces. Yong did not know about this scheme; Hong acted to fund Yong's plan to leave Willowshore.

docs.title: The Party
docs.text: Yong is deeply grateful to the party for saving Hong's life. He offered to refund all the money they'd paid for their weapon orders as thanks—though the party did not take him up on this offer. He owes them a debt that weighs on him.

docs.title: Magistrate Kurosawa
docs.text: Yong clearly resents the workload the magistrate has imposed on him, though he's too cautious to express this openly. He does not want his son to get in trouble with the oni authorities.

docs.title: Plans to Leave
docs.text: Yong privately plans to leave Willowshore. He told Hong he "sees dark skies ahead" for the village and wants to relocate somewhere safer to re-establish his forge. The 100 gold Hong brought home could fund this plan, though Yong is cautious about the windfall—he intends to "pray to our elders and spirits" before deciding whether it's a boon or a darker omen.

docs.title: Services
docs.text:
Yong crafts various metalwork at Eternal Blaze Ironworks:

Item Price Status Polearm (Da Baishan's) 7 gold + shortsword On order — ready by dusk Ji-sarm (Boone's) 8 gold + longsword On order — ready by dusk Shuriken (4) 2 gold Sold to Littlefinger Guandao (Da Baishan's) — Delivered with striking rune Ji-sarm (Boone's) — Delivered with striking rune

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)

  • Took weapon orders from the party
  • His son Hong was trapped in the warehouse fire
  • Offered to refund the party's payments as thanks for saving Hong
  • Hurried away with Hong to avoid the magistrate's attention

  • Promised to have both blades ready by dusk (moved up the timeline)

docs.text:

docs.title: Boone
docs.text: A stranger from distant lands, drawn by curiosity to a town on the edge of change.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: PlayerChris AncestryDwarf ClassFighter BackgroundVeteran, outsider

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Boone is a dwarf—a rarity in Tian Xia, where his kind are almost unheard of. He arrived in Willowshore as a stranger wandering in from distant lands, his presence drawing curious and sometimes suspicious glances from locals who have never seen his like.

His children are grown. His old life is behind him. He's out looking for adventure, and rumors of a new oni taking control of this quiet village drew his curiosity. He wandered in to see what was happening—and found himself in the middle of something far more complex than he expected.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Boone is direct and uncomplicated in a way that contrasts sharply with Willowshore's web of secrets and unspoken traditions. He is not a diplomat. He has no idea what's really happening with the oni and the ruling family's tensions. But he knows how to fight, and he's been itching to get back into the swing of things after years of peaceful retirement.

He likes hitting things. It's been a while, but some skills never fade.

docs.title: Background
docs.text: Boone is a veteran of a war fought long ago—the same conflict that Ginkgo and Donkey remember with their private memorial. But Boone was on the opposite side. None of them feel like winners. The war was like that—nobody came out ahead, and everyone carries the weight of what was lost.

docs.title: Why He Came to Willowshore
docs.text:
Willowshore had been quiet for decades—a declining village that the wider world had largely forgotten. Then word spread that an oni mayor was coming, claiming power after generations of absence. Boone heard the rumors and grew curious. What kind of change was coming to this forgotten place?

He wandered in to find out. He's still figuring out the answer.

docs.title: Ginkgo & Donkey
docs.text: Boone, Ginkgo, and Donkey are all veterans of the same war—though Boone fought on the opposing side. Despite this, they share an understanding that transcends old battle lines. All three see themselves as losers in a conflict that had no real winners. It was their Vietnam.

  • Arrived in Willowshore drawn by curiosity about the oni transition

  • Compared the Kaizo dagger to his own Vestri clan dagger; identified the "endurance" rune as the family motto
  • Helped bury Kaizo's bones at the graveyard

docs.title: Da Baishan
docs.text: "The mountain does not rush, does not shout, and does not move without cause."

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: PlayerBraedon AncestryOrc ClassInvestigator BackgroundDetective among the orc clans

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Da Baishan (大白山, "Big White Mountain") is an orc investigator who grew up in the cavern networks where the orc clans of Chu Ye carved their homes into the bones of the earth. Unlike many of his kin, Baishan was educated deliberately—learning clan histories, ancestral customs, and the unwritten laws that kept blood feuds from becoming extinction events.

Among his people, strength is respected, but endurance is revered. Baishan learned early that the strongest orc is not the one who strikes first, but the one who understands why the strike is coming.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Baishan is scholarly and observant—somewhat surprising for his brutish appearance. He trusts patterns over proclamations and approaches problems methodically. When investigating, he doesn't ask who is guilty—he asks who benefited, who stayed silent, and who changed their habits afterward.

He does not threaten. He explains. And when the truth surfaces, as it always does, Baishan is already standing where it will break.

docs.title: Why He Came to Willowshore
docs.text:
When the oni appeared—after generations of absence—and overthrew the old ruling order, Baishan did not celebrate. Power that arrives suddenly and claims inevitability always hides a cost. While others accepted the new hierarchy, Baishan watched. He listened. And when answers did not come, he left the caverns to investigate for himself.

The trail led him to Willowshore. He finds the oni's "I've always been here" posture deeply suspicious and seeks to uncover the truth behind their sudden return.

docs.title: Littlefinger
docs.text: Baishan caught Littlefinger committing a crime against the oni—cleanly, unmistakably. The evidence was sound, the motive obvious. Rather than turn him in, Baishan recognized a shared suspicion. An alliance formed, not from trust, but from aligned doubt.

docs.title: Ginkgo
docs.text: By the river, Baishan witnessed something that unsettled him deeply: a spirit killing a mortal in full view of the living world. He and Ginkgo stood as witnesses. Ginkgo's leshy beliefs—that death fed life, that decay was not corruption but transformation—stood in stark opposition to Willowshore's funerary laws. Against local custom, Baishan agreed to hide the body in the forest and allow it to return to the cycle. It was a calculated risk. One he still weighs.

docs.title: Donkey
docs.text: At a tavern, Baishan crossed paths with Donkey, an ancient elf who spoke a truth so carefully framed that it led Baishan into public embarrassment. The statement was technically accurate—and deeply misleading. Baishan does not forget such things. Deception masked as wisdom is still deception. Yet he respects Donkey's knowledge and considers him a valuable, if unreliable, source of information.

  • Saw the river spirit during and after combat—the same body he and Ginkgo hid; it shielded him from the cat's attack
  • Heard the spirit's message: "Something stolen, something broken"
  • Spirit lore check (15) found no spirit realm connection to the Mourndusk Willow

docs.title: Session Three (2026-02-12)
docs.text:

  • Retrieved the clay bowl from the ritual pit between the tree's roots—held it through the entire combat, one-handed
  • Examined the ceremonial dagger (Perception 25); identified Toanin inscriptions (Occultism 21): "Let ancient law be sealed in the hand that guides Willowshore"
  • Used Imperial Lore (24) to confirm Toanin is a common oni dialect; "the hand that guides" references the magistrate
  • Investigated the ritual (Occultism 24): identified it as a dark sacrificial substitution ritual—a summoning or communion, at least partially successful
  • Assisted Ginkgo in crafting a salve bomb using his alchemist toolkit (Crafting 21)
  • Abandoned his crossbow after every shot missed; switched to short sword and landed multiple hits on the bear while flanking with Boone
  • Leapt over his allies to tackle and restrain an infected squirrel (Athletics success), then detonated the salve bomb to cure both squirrels

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:

  • Discovered a dwarven skeleton and clan dagger (Kaizo clan, "endurance" rune) in the well's mud
  • Used Odd Feet (investigator feat) to notice the body had been stripped of armor—placed deliberately, not a fall

  • Radiant Willow has matchmaking plans for him with Peony Switchstep, a cheerful seamstress

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Whispered to Littlefinger to hide and search, then crept toward the estate's front door at half speed (Stealth 18, 19—one creaky floorboard and a knocked-over object along the way)
  • Emerged outside and bluffed Magistrate Kurosawa at the front door: "I heard the alarm—I'm here to help" (Deception 12—not great, but Kurosawa's grip on his sword loosened)
  • Helpfully pointed out the shed door was ajar, redirecting Kurosawa's attention to investigate there
  • Dismissed by the magistrate; reconvened with the party outside the estate walls
  • During martial law, helped lead the distraction team to confront Kurosawa directly, offering investigative services
  • Kurosawa told him pointedly: "I believe our criminals are within my sight"—then said he would summon Da Baishan in the morning for investigative assistance
  • Escorted back to Silver Mist Inn under guard
  • Requested an alibi from Migo; the Yeshou family agreed to vouch for the party
  • Level 2 upgrades: Skill increase in Thievery; Shared Stratagem (class feat—ally also treats target as off-guard when using Devise a Stratagem); Eyes of the City (skill feat—use local citizens to help track people in settlements)
  • Peony Switchstep gave Willow visible resistance when she tried to arrange a meeting at the festival

docs.title: Donkey
docs.text: Nobody knows why he's called Donkey. Including himself.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: PlayerDavid AncestryElf (ancient, appears human) ClassWizard BackgroundVillage witch, herbalist

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Donkey is impossibly old. Nobody knows exactly how old—there's definitely some human back in the woodpile somewhere, and people aren't really sure if he's an elf or a human anymore. He's been around Willowshore forever. The name "Donkey" just came about at some point; he didn't correct people, they didn't ask, and it stuck.

Nobody really knows where he lives. He seems to just live off the land, appearing in town every so often to trade herbs and offer help to those who need it. He's something of a village witch—everyone seems to have had at least one conversation with him, though he doesn't talk much.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Donkey speaks rarely, but when he does, his words carry weight—though not always in the way the listener expects. He has a habit of telling truths that are technically accurate but carefully framed to lead people exactly where he wants them to go. Whether this is mischief, wisdom, or something else entirely remains unclear.

He observes more than he participates, watching the town's affairs with the patience of someone who has seen cycles repeat many times over.

docs.title: Role in Willowshore
docs.text:
Donkey serves as an informal resource for the village:

  • Herbalist — Brings herbs from the forest to trade
  • Advisor — Helps those who seek him out, in his own way
  • Living memory — Knows everyone and their family histories through sheer longevity

docs.title: Ginkgo
docs.text: Donkey and Ginkgo share a private tradition—a memorial for a war they both fought in. Once a year, they take time away to remember those who fell. The details remain between them.

docs.title: Boone
docs.text: Boone fought on the opposite side of the same war. Despite this, there's a shared understanding among all three veterans—none of them feel like winners. The war left its mark on everyone.

docs.title: Da Baishan
docs.text:
Donkey has become one of Da Baishan's sources of information in Willowshore. However, their relationship is complicated. Donkey once told Da Baishan something that was technically true but incomplete, leading the investigator into a dangerous situation. Da Baishan emerged, but he's "still slightly miffed" and doesn't fully trust the old wizard—even as he respects his knowledge.

"You want to play hardball in Willowshore? I just wanted to see if you got what it takes."

docs.title: Session Zero (2026-01-16)
docs.text:
• Character created
  • Established as an ancient elf wizard serving as Willowshore's village witch
  • Revealed shared war history with Ginkgo (same side) and Boone (opposing side)
  • Established as an information source for Da Baishan (with a history of "technically true" misdirection)

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text: • Was ill and stayed in his quarters; missed the warehouse fire and recruitment

docs.title: Session Two (2026-02-05)
docs.text:
• Rejoined the party at Silver Mist Lodge after recovering from illness
  • Used Diplomacy (23) to stop Hong from fleeing; commanded him with authority as a known local
  • Identified the Yeshou family as "the most money" in town via Society check
  • Used Willowshore Lore (18) to connect the Mourndusk Willow's glen to a tragedy in the Yeshou family's past
  • Cast Tangle Vine on the ghoul (success—immobilized with -10 speed)
  • Cast Ignition on the zombie (5 fire damage)
  • Learned that Daze doesn't work on mindless undead
  • Picked up a cricket cage during combat for its defensive bonuses
  • Examined a gold coin from Hong's payment—confirmed standard local currency
  • Confirmed the undead rising is unprecedented for Willowshore

docs.title: Session Three (2026-02-12)
docs.text:
• Confirmed the Dark Woods felt different and unnatural—colder, with a presence that wasn't there before, despite years of familiarity
  • Identified the river spirit as Cassian Voss via Willowshore Lore (26)—a religious researcher from Absalom who studied "old gods" and visited Willowshore months before the magistrate
  • Used Spell Substitution to swap a spell slot for Detect Magic (10-minute preparation)
  • Attempted Hydraulic Push with salve to blast the infected bear (missed)
  • Cast Tangle Vine on the bear (missed)
  • Went to 0 HP from squirrel bites and a bear claw; stabilized and healed by Ginkgo
  • Visited Migo with Ginkgo to share findings about the ritual; learned about the Mother of a Thousand Wings and the ley line convergence beneath Willowshore
  • Learned about two secret entrances to the Yeshou estate (stables → wine cellar; koi pond)

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Received a spa treatment from Radiant Willow—soft skin, leafy hair adornments
  • Casually told Willow he'd be leaving for a foraging trip soon and wouldn't be at the ceremony—establishing his alibi
  • Visited Liwen the florist; learned that Magistrate Kurosawa ordered five dozen white lilies (funeral flowers), calling it an "oni custom"
  • Used Spell Substitution to swap a spell during the morning (10-minute preparation)
  • Received fortune petal: "Carry hope in your pocket and every path will bloom brighter."
  • Infiltrated the Yeshou estate by casting Pest Form (firefly); flew to the second-floor master bedroom through an open window
  • Searched the bedroom (Perception 7—couldn't find an office; Perception 21—successfully scanned the desk)
  • Found papers in Chthonic script (the Devil's tongue)—same language as the Mourndusk Willow dagger—along with diagrams
  • Spotted a fox construct (Arcana 9—identified only as "some type of construct") that activated and locked onto him
  • Used Telekinetic Hand to grab the fox and fling it out the window (Wizard DC 17 vs. its Athletics—success)
  • The fox howled a deafening alarm before clearing the window
  • Transformed back from firefly, grabbed the papers from the desk
  • Reached Level 2

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Stuffed three papers from the magistrate's desk into his backpack (Dexterity check—poor, managed only three)
  • Won initiative (15) and completed Pest Form (firefly) just as Kurosawa burst through the bedroom door—escaped through the window with seconds to spare
  • Flew over the estate grounds, saw Kurosawa head to the shed and Da Baishan walk away; transformed back in a copse of trees
  • Identified the stolen papers: a Chthonic journal entry with recent date markings (Decipher Writing 19), an arcane Web scroll (2nd level), and a map with a marking ~10 miles east of Willowshore featuring a diagram resembling the device Yong was building for the magistrate
  • Used Read Aura cantrip to confirm Shang Yesho's military medal is not magical
  • Spoke with Luda, his regular arcane supplier; learned Kurosawa demanded high-volume, specific arcane ingredients from her
  • Asked Luda to track and report future purchases by the magistrate; she agreed
  • Tested the martial law perimeter by walking toward the edge of town; confronted by an oni guard with a drawn sword
  • Used Conceal Spell (new wizard feat) to cast Sleep without visible components (DC 18; guard rolled 17—failure); the guard toppled unconscious
  • "Remember to meddle not in the affairs of wizards."
  • Level 2 upgrades: Conceal Spell (class feat—spells gain the Subtle trait), Tattoo Artist (skill feat—can inscribe magical tattoos)
  • Homebrew hero point: Improvise Spell—cast any spell in spellbook even if not memorized, once per session

docs.title: Ginkgo
docs.text: "Death is not an ending. It is a return to the cycle."

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: PlayerOded AncestryLeshy (Fungus) ClassCleric BackgroundScout and information gatherer

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Ginkgo is a fungus leshy who serves as a scout, collecting information about the oni presence in Chu Ye. As a leshy, he is connected to other members of his kind through the mycelium network—a vast underground web of fungal communication that spans the forests.

His fungal nature gives him a unique perspective on life and death. To Ginkgo, decay is not corruption but transformation. Death feeds life. Everything is part of one great ecosystem, endlessly cycling.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Ginkgo approaches the world through the lens of ecology and natural cycles. He is uncertain about spirits—their existence doesn't quite fit with his worldview—but he cannot deny that something is happening in Willowshore that defies easy explanation.

He is pragmatic about death in ways that others find unsettling. Where villagers see tragedy, Ginkgo sees the continuation of the cycle.

docs.title: Beliefs
docs.text:
Ginkgo's philosophy centers on the interconnectedness of all living things:

  • Death is transformation — Bodies should return to the earth, feeding new life
  • The mycelium connects all — Through the fungal network, information and nutrients flow between all living things
  • Spirits are uncertain — He neither fully believes nor disbelieves, but observes

docs.title: Donkey
docs.text: Ginkgo and Donkey share a private tradition—a memorial for a battle they both fought in long ago. On this day each year, they take time away from others to remember. The details of this war remain private between them.

docs.title: Boone
docs.text: Boone fought on the opposite side of the same war that Ginkgo and Donkey commemorate. There are no winners in their shared history—all three consider themselves on the losing side. This creates an unusual bond of mutual understanding among embittered veterans.

docs.title: Da Baishan
docs.text: By the river one night, Ginkgo and Da Baishan witnessed a spirit kill a person. According to local legend, death by a spirit means the body could return or be controlled unless disposed of through special rites. Ginkgo, following his beliefs about the natural cycle, convinced Da Baishan to help hide the body in the forest instead—allowing it to decompose naturally and return to the earth. This shared secret binds them.

docs.title: Session Zero (2026-01-16)
docs.text:
• Character created
  • Established as a fungus leshy cleric connected to the mycelium
  • Revealed shared war history with Donkey (same side) and Boone (opposing side)
  • Witnessed a spirit killing with Da Baishan; hid the body against local custom

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:
• Helped rescue children from the burning warehouse; used Message cantrip to guide Hong to safety
  • Applied Da Baishan's burn salve to the injured boy
  • Provided Guidance to allies throughout the rescue
  • Cast Heal on Littlefinger after the rescue
  • Met Radiant Willow, a fellow leshy; scheduled for a makeover before the festival
  • Recruited as a deputy by Magistrate Kurosawa

docs.title: Session Two (2026-02-05)
docs.text:
• Cast Guidance to help allies during Hong's interrogation
  • Offered to accompany Hong to the Yeshou family; shuffled to block the view when Littlefinger palmed a coin
  • Identified the Mourndusk Willow via Nature check (21)—ancient, rare, linked to dark magics but coexisting harmlessly with Willowshore for generations
  • Cast Bless during the graveyard combat, providing +1 to attack rolls for the party; sustained and expanded it to 25-foot radius
  • Attempted Divine Lance against the undead cat (missed)
  • Saw the river spirit—the same body he and Da Baishan hid—reaching out for help
  • Religion check identified the first creature as a ghoul (feeds on recently dead flesh)

docs.title: Session Three (2026-02-12)
docs.text:
• Tasted the earth upon entering the Dark Woods (Nature 8—failed to gather useful info, but sensed an unnatural presence through his bare feet)
  • Examined the yellow lichen blight on the Mourndusk Willow (Nature 25): identified it as an arcane blight—a "bruise" or after-effect of an energy blast, not a natural growth
  • Communed with the tree and felt it was "sad and battered"
  • Scraped the lichen and observed spores releasing into the wind; confirmed the blight was surface-level, not deeply infecting the bark
  • Created a healing salve from his own body—tearing off a piece of his fungal self, mixing it with healer's toolkit ingredients. Applied it to the tree, turning the lichen gray and neutralizing it
  • Begged Boone not to kill the infected bear: "Please don't kill it. Please. Just incapacitate it."
  • Cast Fear on the bear (frightened 2, -2 to all checks/saves/AC)
  • Cast Heal on Boone twice (11 HP and 12 HP) to bring him back from unconsciousness
  • Cast Stabilize on Boone when he was dying
  • Healed Donkey with Treat Wounds (Medicine 20, 9 HP)
  • Attempted to grab and cure a squirrel (failed grapple checks)
  • Crafted additional salve during combat for the salve bomb
  • Spoke a farewell to the fallen bear: "The soil welcomes you. By tomorrow the forest will know your name."
  • Led the effort to cleanse the entire tree of blight using the salve
  • Visited Migo with Donkey to share findings; learned about the Mother of a Thousand Wings and ley lines beneath Willowshore
  • Enjoyed Migo's rice cakes (again)

docs.title: Session Four (2026-02-26)
docs.text:
• Received a peach petal exfoliation and makeover from Radiant Willow—flower petals woven into his fungal exterior, leaving him "glistening in the light of day"
  • Flirted with Willow, telling her about the leshy belief that five successful matches earn a place in heaven; said "maybe you and I are a match" then immediately apologized for being unsubtle
  • Willow agreed to get a drink with him later
  • Recruited by Willow as a matchmaking accomplice for Da Baishan, Boone, and Littlefinger
  • Learned from Willow that Cassian Voss spoke of "five lines, five fortunes" and that "something is being drawn here"
  • Received fortune petal: "Even the tiniest bud dreams of becoming a blossom. So should you."
  • Stayed at the festival during the infiltration; maintained Message communication with Da Baishan
  • Watched the ceremonial dance; observed the moth swarm destroy five lanterns (Perception 25)
  • Noticed Magistrate Kurosawa and Migo lock eyes after the moth incident
  • Tapped into the mycelium network (Nature 27) to eavesdrop on the magistrate and mayor's conversation—overheard the mayor's complaint about missing food
  • Helped Boone stall Mayor Masru with food to buy the infiltration team time
  • Cast Guidance on Boone's diplomacy attempt (it didn't help—Boone rolled a 3)
  • Reached Level 2

docs.title: Session Five (2026-03-12)
docs.text:
• Reunited with the party outside the estate walls after the escape; remarked on Da Baishan's sweat and Littlefinger's soaking
  • Proposed splitting up during martial law: go talk to the magistrate as a distraction while Littlefinger hides the contraband
  • Part of the distraction team that confronted Magistrate Kurosawa: backed up Boone's deception—"He hired us. He paid us gold. We're reporting back to him."
  • Escorted back to Silver Mist Inn by oni guards
  • Visited Migo with the party; learned about the Gosembiki (Five Pointed Star)—haunted ruins east of Willowshore
  • Responded to Migo's question about the mayor: "Unbelievably friendly."
  • Lit his pipe with the martial law decree
  • Rolled poorly on the Society check at the festival (distracted—"very distracted by maybe the drink or other things")
  • Level 2 upgrades: Communal Healing (class feat—when healing one character, also heal an adjacent character); skill feat TBD
  • Homebrew hero point: Divine Surge—instantly heal self or one ally in range, OR regain a focus point / re-roll a failed save

docs.title: Littlefinger
docs.text: A thief with a conscience—or at least, a thief with standards.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
PlayerVic AncestryHalfling ClassRogue BackgroundEager Scofflaw; connected to the ruling family

Name TBD

"Littlefinger" is a placeholder name. The character's true name will be revealed in play.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Littlefinger is a halfling rogue with connections to Willowshore's ruling family—whether through marriage, blood, or some other tie remains to be seen. When the family fell on hard times and their influence waned, he turned to thievery to survive.

But he's not just any thief. Littlefinger fancies himself a Robin Hood type: stealing from the oni and their collaborators to help the common folk of Willowshore. There's never an autocrat for good, they say—but maybe there can be a thief for good.

docs.title: Personality
docs.text:
Littlefinger is cunning and opportunistic, with halfling luck on his side. He's drawn to schemes and capers, always looking for the next mark—preferably someone who deserves to lose what they have.

He has a strong sense that the oni are bad for Willowshore, and he's willing to take risks to undermine them however he can.

docs.title: Skills & Abilities
docs.text:
As an Eager Scofflaw, Littlefinger has honed a particular set of skills:

  • Halfling Luck — Fortune favors the bold (and the small)
  • Sneak Attack — Strike from the shadows
  • Thieves' Tools — No lock is truly secure
  • Deception & Stealth — The art of not being where people think you are

docs.title: Da Baishan
docs.text: Da Baishan caught Littlefinger in the act—breaking into something connected to the oni. The evidence was clear, the crime undeniable. But instead of turning him in, Da Baishan recognized a kindred suspicion of the new regime. They formed an alliance, not from trust, but from aligned purpose. Forged in crime.

docs.title: The Ruling Family
docs.text: Littlefinger has ties to Willowshore's de facto rulers—the family that refuses to participate in the Revival of Friendship for the new oni mayor. The exact nature of this connection (marriage, blood, employment) remains unclear.

docs.title: Session Zero (2026-01-16)
docs.text:
• Character created
  • Established as a halfling rogue with ties to the ruling family
  • Caught stealing from oni-related targets by Da Baishan; formed an alliance
  • Expressed interest in Robin Hood-style theft to help Willowshore's common folk

docs.title: Session One (2026-01-30)
docs.text:
• Led the way into the burning warehouse despite misgivings
  • Took damage from exploding fireworks and falling shelves
  • Used Halfling Luck to save Hong; threw himself over the boy to shield him from debris
  • Purchased shuriken from Yong; traded dagger to Da Baishan for an orcish sap
  • Recruited as a deputy by Magistrate Kurosawa
  • Booked a room at Silver Mist Lodge under his name
  • Plans to use his connections to investigate the ruling family

docs.title: Session Two (2026-02-05)
docs.text:
